concept: Trust / A Deliberate Opening of the Self
description: The confident reliance on the integrity and goodwill of another. It is a courageous and considered choice to make one's inner self vulnerable and whole in relation to another.
rationale: Composed from the established roots 'the' (deliberate consideration), 'o' (wholeness), and 'mi' (the inner self). The word's structure suggests 'a deliberate wholeness of the self.' This is a more profound and personal version than our previous 'theo', framing trust as a deeply considered, internal act of opening oneself completely.
